edges.description: Alertmanager forwards warning, info, and mirrored critical notifications into Mattermost channels, but operators can still inspect active alerts directly if chat delivery is degraded.
Critical Alertmanager notifications publish into the governed ntfy topic registry for operator push delivery, while Mattermost still provides the team-visible fallback channel.
The unified API gateway validates caller JWTs against Keycloak before serving authenticated platform routes.
The structured-search endpoints delegate keyword and facet queries to the private Typesense runtime when it is available.
Public access to api.example.com depends on the shared edge publication path, but the internal gateway remains reachable directly.
Authenticated operator access to the browser runner uses the governed API gateway route, while the private runtime can still be verified locally.
The private Playwright browser runner is hosted on docker-runtime.
Authenticated operator and automation access to Changedetection is brokered through the governed API gateway route, while the private runtime remains directly verifiable on docker-runtime.
The Changedetection.io runtime and its datastore volume are hosted on docker-runtime.
Repo-managed watch groups route platform-ops notifications through the existing Mattermost webhook manifest.
Security-advisory watches publish repo-managed operator push notifications through the governed ntfy channel.
Validation-gate failures publish critical operator push notifications to ntfy without requiring UI polling.
The ntfy runtime, topic registry, and auth state are hosted on docker-runtime.
Public operator access to ntfy.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path, while the private runtime remains directly verifiable.
The repo-seeded Windmill resource and NATS bridge publish high-priority operator push notifications through ntfy.
The Crawl4AI runtime and its bounded headless-browser worker pool are hosted on docker-runtime.
The changelog portal is served as generated static content on nginx.
Authenticated browser access to coolify.example.com depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, while the private controller remains reachable over the management network.
Public dashboard access to coolify.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication, while the private controller proxy remains reachable directly.
The shared applications ingress is configured and served by the Coolify runtime and its managed proxy.
Public access to apps.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication while the guest-local ingress remains reachable for direct verification.
Dify uses the governed API gateway route to call named LV3 tools, but the canvas remains reachable without tool invocation.
Operator browser access to Dify is gated behind the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak authentication flow at the edge.
Dify can publish workflow traces into Langfuse, but authoring and bootstrap access remain available without trace export.
Public reachability for agents.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Dify can use the private Ollama runtime for local model inference, but the canvas can still operate with external model providers.
Dify loads its mirrored database, admin, and tool-bridge secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Dify stores application state, accounts, knowledge metadata, and workflow definitions in Postgres.
The generated developer portal is published as static content on nginx.
Authenticated browser access to logs.example.com depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, but the private hub remains reachable for controlled operator verification.
Public operator access to logs.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication while the private runtime remains reachable inside the guest network.
The Excalidraw frontend and collaboration room are hosted on docker-runtime.
Operator access to draw.example.com depends on the shared Keycloak-backed oauth2-proxy edge flow, while the private runtime can still be verified locally.
The private diagram surface is published at draw.example.com through the shared NGINX edge, including the routed /socket.io/ collaboration path.
The Flagsmith Community Edition runtime and task processor are hosted on docker-runtime.
Browser access to flags.example.com is gated by the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak edge-auth path, while private environment-key consumers continue to use the internal runtime directly.
Public reachability for flags.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Flagsmith consumes mirrored runtime secrets and publishes seeded environment API keys through the OpenBao-backed compose environment path.
Flagsmith stores organisations, projects, environments, feature state, and audit metadata in the shared PostgreSQL cluster.
Gitea Actions jobs are delegated to the dedicated runner on docker-build, but repository hosting remains available without the runner.
Operator SSO into Gitea prefers Keycloak, while the repo-managed bootstrap admin remains the break-glass fallback.
Gitea loads its mirrored database, webhook, and OIDC client secrets from the OpenBao sidecar before startup.
Gitea persists repositories, metadata, and issue state in Postgres.
Git LFS object reads and writes depend on the shared MinIO bucket, while repository hosting and metadata remain available without new LFS traffic.
Authenticated operator and cross-service access can be brokered through the shared API gateway route, while private guest-network callers can still use the internal listener directly.
The Gotenberg Chromium and LibreOffice renderers are hosted on runtime-ai.
Grafana uses Keycloak for the preferred SSO path, but local admin remains the break-glass fallback.
Public access to grafana.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge; private reachability remains available if the edge is degraded.
Loki chunk persistence now depends on the shared MinIO bucket, while metrics and trace dashboards can still serve partial observability if object storage is degraded.
Operator browser sign-in to registry.example.com depends on the shared Keycloak OIDC path, while robot credentials still support governed automation.
Public reachability for registry.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Public registration and control-plane reachability for headscale.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Public operator access to Homepage is intentionally gated through the shared Keycloak-backed oauth2-proxy flow on the NGINX edge.
The unified dashboard is published at home.example.com through the shared NGINX edge and is not exposed directly on the public internet.
Staging and non-production Keycloak runs can relay SMTP through Mailpit for assertion-level mail verification without sending real email.
Public reachability for sso.example.com depends on the shared edge publication path.
Keycloak reads its mirrored database and bootstrap secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Keycloak stores realm, client, and session state in Postgres.
Keycloak depends on the internal certificate authority when repo-managed internal TLS material is refreshed.
Keycloak provides the preferred OIDC browser sign-in path, but a repo-managed bootstrap user remains the break-glass fallback.
Public access to langfuse.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Langfuse event export and media attachment flows depend on the shared MinIO bucket, while core trace and project metadata remain available from Postgres and ClickHouse.
Langfuse starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Langfuse stores application metadata, users, projects, and bootstrap state in Postgres.
LiveKit runs as a repo-managed Docker Compose workload on docker-runtime.
Public signalling at livekit.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
LiveKit reads its API key pair from the OpenBao-backed runtime env contract before the service is verified.
Public API and console reachability depend on the shared NGINX edge publication path, while the private guest-network listeners remain reachable directly.
MinIO starts from OpenBao-rendered root credentials mirrored into its compose environment before bucket and policy reconciliation runs.
The published mail.example.com informational surface is exposed through the shared NGINX edge.
The mail gateway runtime is rendered from secrets mirrored locally by the OpenBao sidecar.
Redpanda loads its mirrored admin and platform principal secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Public reachability for matrix.example.com depends on the shared edge publication path.
Matrix Synapse starts from mirrored database credentials rendered through the shared OpenBao compose environment helper.
Matrix Synapse stores users, room metadata, media references, and event history in Postgres.
Mattermost starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Mattermost stores channels, users, and message history in Postgres.
The public n8n editor path is gated behind oauth2-proxy and Keycloak-backed browser authentication.
Public reachability for n8n.example.com depends on the shared edge publication path.
n8n reads its mirrored database password and encryption key through the OpenBao agent before startup.
n8n stores workflow, credential, and execution state in Postgres.
NetBox receives its database and Redis credentials from the OpenBao agent before the application containers start.
NetBox stores all inventory, topology, and IPAM data in Postgres.
Neko WebRTC signalling is proxied through the shared NGINX edge at browser.example.com with TLS termination and WebSocket upgrade support.
Neko operator access is controlled through Keycloak OIDC client configuration for Tailscale headscale management network.
Public reachability for cloud.example.com and DAV discovery redirects depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Nextcloud receives its mirrored database, Redis, and admin bootstrap secrets through the OpenBao agent before startup.
Nextcloud stores account, sharing, file, and app metadata in Postgres.
Long-running internal helper services pinned to the build pool depend on the docker-build Nomad client remaining available for placement.
Runtime-targeted batch jobs and internal helper workloads depend on the docker-runtime Nomad client remaining available for placement.
LiteLLM Proxy forwards local chat and embedding aliases to the private Ollama runtime.
LiteLLM Proxy stores virtual keys and usage data in PostgreSQL.
LiteLLM Proxy consumes runtime secrets from the OpenBao sidecar.
LibreChat connects directly to Ollama for local model inference.
LibreChat uses Keycloak OIDC for authentication but can fall back to local login.
LibreChat consumes runtime secrets from the OpenBao sidecar.
LibreChat delegates optional web-search requests to the private SearXNG runtime.
The repo-managed bootstrap verification path checks Keycloak-issued operator and runtime identities alongside the delegated authorization graph.
OpenFGA reads its mirrored datastore password and preshared API key from the OpenBao-backed runtime secret path before startup.
OpenFGA stores authorization stores, models, and relationship tuples in PostgreSQL.
The Typesense runtime and its derived index volume are hosted on docker-runtime.
Typesense reads the mirrored API key from the OpenBao-backed runtime secret path before startup.
The private operator JWT route at /v1/label-studio is published through the shared API gateway on docker-runtime.
The Label Studio runtime container and guest-local listener are hosted on docker-runtime.
Label Studio persists project metadata, task state, and annotations in the dedicated PostgreSQL database label_studio on postgres.
The shared NGINX edge publishes annotate.example.com and enforces the browser auth boundary ahead of Label Studio.
Browser access to annotate.example.com is gated by the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak edge-auth flow, while app-local Label Studio tokens remain the current automation path.
Label Studio consumes OpenBao-delivered runtime secrets during converge and container startup.
Governed operator API access to Paperless flows through the shared platform API gateway.
Paperless runs as a compose-managed workload on docker-runtime.
Paperless delegates routine browser sign-in to the shared Keycloak OIDC broker.
Public access to paperless.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Paperless starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Paperless stores document metadata, workflow state, and taxonomy in Postgres.
Temporal stores workflow history, task queues, and visibility records in PostgreSQL.
Temporal reads its mirrored PostgreSQL password from the OpenBao-backed runtime secret path before startup.
The interactive operations portal depends on the unified API gateway for governed platform actions and live status reads.
ops.example.com is gated behind oauth2-proxy and Keycloak-backed browser authentication.
The generated portal is published as static content on nginx.
Repo-intake is hosted on the shared docker-runtime guest.
repo-intake.example.com is gated behind the shared edge auth boundary and Keycloak-backed browser authentication.
Public access to repo-intake.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Grist delegates routine browser sign-in to the shared Keycloak OIDC broker.
Public access to grist.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Grist starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Outline delegates routine browser sign-in to the shared Keycloak OIDC broker.
Public access to wiki.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Outline starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment.
Outline stores workspace, user, and document metadata in Postgres.
Public producer ingest at billing.example.com/api/v1/events is brokered by the platform API gateway adapter and canonical error-handling path.
The Lago API, front end, worker, clock, Redis, and PDF sidecar run on docker-runtime.
Operator browser access to billing.example.com is gated by the shared oauth2-proxy and Keycloak edge-auth path.
Public reachability for billing.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Lago stores billable metrics, plans, customers, subscriptions, invoices, and aggregated usage state in the shared PostgreSQL cluster.
Authenticated browser access to tasks.example.com depends on the shared Keycloak-backed edge auth path, while the private controller path remains available for governed automation.
Public access to tasks.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Plane consumes mirrored runtime secrets from the OpenBao sidecar before the containers start.
Plane persists workspaces, projects, users, and issue metadata in Postgres.
The platform context API loads its bearer token and runtime configuration from OpenBao-rendered environment files.
Raw document upload staging depends on the shared MinIO bucket, while read-only query access can still run against the indexed corpus if staging is unavailable.
The indexed corpus includes step-ca ADR and runbook content for operator query grounding.
The Plausible runtime, bundled PostgreSQL, and bundled ClickHouse analytics store run on docker-runtime.
SMTP delivery for notifications and password recovery uses the repo-managed server mailbox and internal submission relay on mail-platform.
Dashboard publication at analytics.example.com and tracker injection on selected public hostnames depend on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Plausible starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment before the main container launches.
The Superset runtime and its OpenBao-backed compose stack run on docker-runtime.
Superset persists metadata in the managed superset schema of the shared postgres database and registers read-only PostgreSQL datasources from postgres.
Routine browser sign-in to bi.example.com depends on the repo-managed Keycloak OIDC client and realm configuration.
Public publication at bi.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Superset starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment before the main container launches.
Semaphore loads database and bootstrap secrets from the OpenBao agent before the container starts.
Semaphore persists project metadata, inventories, templates, and task history in Postgres.
ServerClaw prefers Keycloak-backed browser sign-in while retaining a repo-managed bootstrap admin as the break-glass fallback.
Public access to chat.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
ServerClaw routes its default local model connector to the private Ollama runtime on docker-runtime.
ServerClaw consumes mirrored runtime secrets and the dedicated OIDC client secret from the OpenBao sidecar.
ServerClaw delegates optional web-search requests to the private SearXNG runtime when the feature is enabled.
The public status page is published at status.example.com through the shared NGINX edge hostname.
The public status page is rendered by the Uptime Kuma runtime and cannot function if that service is unavailable.
The Apache Tika extraction runtime is hosted on runtime-ai.
Vaultwarden stores encrypted vault metadata and shared credential state in PostgreSQL.
Vaultwarden uses step-ca issued private TLS certificates for the Tailscale-only HTTPS surface and managed renewal path.
Windmill workers and the server runtime load mirrored secrets from the OpenBao agent before the containers start.
Windmill persists workflow metadata, run history, and state in Postgres.
The Directus runtime container and guest-local listener are hosted on docker-runtime.
Directus persists its system state and governed collections in the dedicated PostgreSQL database directus on postgres.
The shared NGINX edge publishes data.example.com directly to Directus on docker-runtime.
Routine browser sign-in delegates to Keycloak via OIDC while the local Directus admin remains break-glass only.
Directus consumes OpenBao-delivered runtime secrets during converge and container startup.
The Tesseract OCR runtime and its repo-built container image are hosted on runtime-ai.
Document-extraction callers invoke Tika first and only fall back to Tesseract OCR when Tika returns no embedded text.
Woodpecker delegates authentication and repository activation to Gitea through the governed OAuth and API bootstrap path.
Public operator access to ci.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge, while the controller path remains reachable on the Proxmox host tailnet.
Woodpecker loads mirrored database, OAuth, and agent secrets from the OpenBao agent before the runtime starts.
Woodpecker persists repository metadata, secrets, and pipeline history in Postgres.
The GlitchTip web runtime, project bootstrap flow, and supporting Valkey container run on docker-runtime.
GlitchTip stores issues, grouped events, and project metadata in the shared PostgreSQL cluster.
GlitchTip starts from OpenBao-rendered runtime secrets mirrored into its compose environment before the main container launches.
GlitchTip delegates browser sign-in to the shared Keycloak OIDC broker through a repo-managed confidential client.
Public access to errors.example.com depends on the shared NGINX edge publication path.
Digest delivery and password-recovery mail use the repo-managed server mailbox and internal submission relay on mail-platform.
New issue alerts are routed to the platform-ops Mattermost webhook manifest.
High-priority regression notifications are delivered through the private ntfy paging gateway.
